name: dnf
description: Package manager for RPM-based Linux distributions (successor to yum)

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: Search the repositories for a package

name: install
in
subcommands.description: Install the given package(s) and its dependencies

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove package(s)

subcommands.name: autoremove
subcommands.description: Removes packages installed as dependencies that are no longer required by currently installed programs

subcommands.name: check-update
subcommands.description: Checks for updates, but does not download or install the packages

subcommands.name: downgrade
subcommands.description: Reverts to the previous version of a package

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Provides basic information about the package including name, version, release, and description

subcommands.name: reinstall
subcommands.description: Reinstalls the currently installed package

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Check the repositories for newer packages and updates them

subcommands.name: exclude
subcommands.description: Exclude a package from the transaction

subcommands.name: alias
subcommands.description: Define and manage a list of aliases

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists all defined aliases

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Create new aliases

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete aliases

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Checks the local packagedb and produces information on any problems it finds

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Performs cleanup of temporary files kept for repositories

subcommands.name: dbcache
subcommands.description: Removes cache files generated from the repository metadata

subcommands.name: expire-cache
subcommands.description: Marks the repository metadata as expired

subcommands.name: metadata
subcommands.description: Removes repository metadata

subcommands.name: packages
subcommands.description: Removes any cached packages from the system

subcommands.name: all
subcommands.description: Does all of the below

name: distro-sync
dsync
subcommands.description: Upgrades, downgrades, or keeps selected installed packages to match the latest version available from any enabled repository

name: downgrade
dg
subcommands.description: Downgrades the specified packages to the highest installable package of all known lower versions

name: group
grp
subcommands.description: Manage groups (virtual collections of packages)

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Display package lists of a group

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Mark the specified group installed and install packages it contains

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List all matching groups

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Mark the group removed and remove the packages in the group that do not belong to another installed group and were not installed explicitly by the user

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrades the packages from the group and upgrades the group itself

subcommands.name: mark
subcommands.description: Mark the specified group

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Mark the specified group installed without actually installing

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Mark the specified group removed without actually removing

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Displays the help text for all commands

name: history
hist
subcommands.description: View what has happened in past transactions

options.name: --reverse
options.description: Print the output in reverse order

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Describe the given transactions

subcommands.name: redo
subcommands.description: Repeat the specified transaction

subcommands.name: replay
subcommands.description: Replay a transaction file stored in file by the history store command

options.name: --ignore-installed
options.description: Don't check for the installed packages being in the same state as those recorded in the transaction

options.name: --ignore-extras
options.description: Don't check for extra packages pulled into the transaction on the target system

options.name: --skip-unavailable
options.description: Skip packages not available on the target system that are stored in the transaction

subcommands.name: rollback
subcommands.description: Undo all transactions performed after the specified transaction

subcommands.name: store
subcommands.description: Store a transaction

name: -o
--output-file
options.description: Store the serialized transaction into the given file

subcommands.name: undo
subcommands.description: Perform the opposite operation to all operations performed in the specified transaction

subcommands.name: userinstalled
subcommands.description: Show all install-only packages outside of DNF and packages not installed as a dependency

name: info
if
subcommands.description: Lists description and summary information about installed and available packages

name: list
ls
subcommands.description: Prints lists of packages

options.name: --all
options.description: Lists all packages, present in the RPMDB, in a repository, or both

options.name: --installed
options.description: Lists installed packages

options.name: --available
options.description: Lists available packages

options.name: --extras
options.description: Lists extras (packages installed on the system that are not available in any known repository

options.name: --obsoletes
options.description: List packages installed on the system that are obsoleted by packages in any known repository

options.name: --recent
options.description: List packages recently added into the repositories

options.name: --upgrades
options.description: Lists upgrades available for the installed packages

options.name: --autoremove
options.description: List packages which will be removed by dnf autoremove

name: makecache
mc
subcommands.description: Downloads and caches metadata for enabled repositories

options.name: --timer
options.description: Instructs DNF to be more resource-aware

subcommands.name: mark
subcommands.description: Mark package(s)

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Marks the given packages as installed by the user

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Unmarks the given packages as installed by the user

subcommands.name: group
subcommands.description: Marks the specified packages as installed by group

subcommands.name: module
subcommands.description: Manage modules

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install module profiles, including their packages

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update packages associated with an active module stream

subcommands.name: switch-to
subcommands.description: Switch to or enable a module stream, change versions of installed packages to versions provided by the new stream, and remove packages from the old stream that are no longer available

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove installed module profiles, including packages that were installed with dnf module install

options.name: --all
options.description: Additionally removes all packages whose names are provided by specified modules

subcommands.name: enable
subcommands.description: Enable a module stream and make the stream RPMs available in the package set

subcommands.name: disable
subcommands.description: Disable a module; all related module streams will become unavailable

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Reset module state so it's no longer enabled/disabled

subcommands.name: provides
subcommands.description: List all modular packages matching the given package name spec

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists module streams

options.name: --all
options.description: Lists all module streams, their profiles and states

options.name: --enabled
options.description: Lists module streams that are enabled

options.name: --disabled
options.description: Lists module streams that are disabled

options.name: --installed
options.description: Lists module streams with installed profiles

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Print detailed information about the given module stream

options.name: --profile
options.description: Print detailed information about the given module profiles

subcommands.name: repoquery
subcommands.description: List all available packages belonging to selected modules

options.name: --available
options.description: List all available packages belonging to selected modules

options.name: --installed
options.description: List all installed packages with the same name like packages belonging to selected modules

name: provides
prov
subcommands.description: Finds the packages providing the given provide spec

name: reinstall
rei
subcommands.description: Installs the specified packages

name: remove
rm
subcommands.description: Remove package(s) along with any packages depending on the packages being removed

options.name: --duplicates
options.description: Removes older versions of duplicate packages

options.name: --oldinstallonly
options.description: Removes old installonly packages

subcommands.name: repoinfo
subcommands.description: Alias for the repolist command

subcommands.name: repolist
subcommands.description: List repositories

options.name: --enabled
options.description: Lists enabled repositories

options.name: --disabled
options.description: Lists disabled repositories

options.name: --all
options.description: Lists all known repositories

name: repoquery
rq
subcommands.description: Searches available DNF repositories and displays the requested information

options.name: --groupmember
options.description: List groups that contain <package-spec>

options.name: --querytags
options.description: Provides the list of tags recognized by the --queryformat repoquery option

name: -a
--all
options.description: Query all packages

name: --arch
--archlist
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages of selected architectures

options.name: --duplicates
options.description: Limit the resulting set to installed duplicate packages

options.name: --unneeded
options.description: Limit the resulting set to leaves packages that were installed as depedencies so they are no longer needed

options.name: --available
options.description: Limit the resulting set to available packages only (set by default)

options.name: --disable-modular-filtering
options.description: Disables filtering of modular packages, so that packages of inactive module streams are included in the result

options.name: --extras
options.description: Limit the resulting set to packages that are not present in any of the available repositories

name: -f
--file
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to the package that owns the given file

options.name: --installed
options.description: Limit the resulting set to installed packages only

options.name: --installonly
options.description: Limit the resulting set to installed installonly packages

options.name: --latest-limit
options.description: Limit the resulting set to the given number of latest packages for every package name and architecture

options.name: --recent
options.description: Limit the resulting set to packages that were recently edited

options.name: --repo
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages from a repository identified by <repoid>

options.name: --unsatisfied
options.description: Report unsatisfied dependencies among installed packages

options.name: --upgrades
options.description: Limit the resulting set to packages that provide an upgrade for some already installed package

options.name: --userinstalled
options.description: Limit the resulting set to packages installed by the user

options.name: --whatdepends
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that require, enhance, recommend, suggest or supplement any of the given capabilities

options.name: --alldeps
options.description: Adds all packages requiring the package features to the result set

options.name: --whatconflicts
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that conflict with any of the given capabilities

options.name: --whatenhances
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that enhance any of the given capabilities

options.name: --whatobsoletes
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that obsolete any of the given capabilities

options.name: --whatprovides
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that provide any of the given capabilities

options.name: --whatrecommends
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that recommend any of the given capabilities

options.name: --whatrequires
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that require any of the given capabilities

options.name: --alldeps
options.description: Adds all packages requiring the package features to the result set

options.name: --exactdeps
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that require the given capability

options.name: --whatsuggests
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that suggest any of the given capabilities

options.name: --whatsupplements
options.description: Limit the resulting set only to packages that supplement any of the given capabilities

options.name: --srpm
options.description: Operate on the corresponding source RPM

name: -i
--info
options.description: Show detailed information about the package

name: -l
--list
options.description: Show the list of files in the package

name: -s
--source
options.description: Show the package source RPM name

options.name: --changelogs
options.description: Print the package changelogs

options.name: --conflicts
options.description: Display capabilities that the package conflicts with

options.name: --depends
options.description: Display capabilities that the package depends on, enhances, recommends, suggests, or supplements

options.name: --enhances
options.description: Display capabilities enhanced by the package

name: --location
options.description: Show a location where the package could be downloaded from

name: --obsoletes
options.description: Display capabilities that the package obsoletes

name: --provides
options.description: Display capabilities provided by the package

name: --recommends
options.description: Display capabilities recommended by the package

name: --requires
options.description: Display capabilities that the package depends on

name: --requires-pre
options.description: Display capabilities that the package depends on for running a %pre script

name: --suggests
options.description: Display capabilities suggested by the package

name: --supplements
options.description: Display capabilities supplemented by the package

name: --tree
options.description: Display a recursive tree of packages with capabilities specified by one of the following supplementary options: --whatrequires, --requires, --conflicts, --enhances, --suggests, --provides, --supplements, --recommends.

name: --deplist
options.description: Produce a list of all direct dependencies and what packages provide those dependencies for the given packages

name: --nvr
options.description: Show found packages in the name-version-release format

name: --nevra
options.description: Show found packages in the name-epoch:version-release.architecture format

name: --envra
options.description: Show found packages in the epoch:name-version-release.architecture format

name: --qf
--queryformat
options.description: Custom display format

name: --recursive
options.description: Query packages recursively. Has to be used with --whatrequires <REQ> (optionally with --alldeps, but not with --exactdeps) or with --requires <REQ> --resolve.

name: --resolve
options.description: Resolve capabilities to originating package(s)

subcommands.name: repository-packages
subcommands.description: Run commands on top of all packages in the given repository

subcommands.name: check-update
subcommands.description: Non-interactively checks if updates of the specified packages in the repository are available

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: List packages

options.name: --all
options.description: List all related packages

options.name: --installed
options.description: List packages installed from the repository

options.name: --available
options.description: List packages available in the repository but not currently installed on the system

options.name: --extras
options.description: List packages installed from the repository that are not available in any repository

options.name: --obsoletes
options.description: List packages in the repository that obsolete packages installed on the system

options.name: --recent
options.description: List packages recently added into the repository

options.name: --upgrades
options.description: List packages in the repository that upgrade packages installed on the system

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install packages matching the given package spec from the repository

options.name: --all
options.description: List all related packages

options.name: --installed
options.description: List packages installed from the repository

options.name: --available
options.description: List packages available in the repository but not currently installed on the system

options.name: --extras
options.description: List packages installed from the repository that are not available in any repository

options.name: --obsoletes
options.description: List packages in the repository that obsolete packages installed on the system

options.name: --recent
options.description: List packages recently added into the repository

options.name: --upgrades
options.description: List packages in the repository that upgrade packages installed on the system

subcommands.name: move-to
subcommands.description: Reinstall all those packages that are available in the repository

subcommands.name: reinstall
subcommands.description: Run the reinstall-old subcommand. If it fails, run the move-to subcommand

subcommands.name: reinstall-old
subcommands.description: Reinstall all those packages that were installed from the repository and simultaneously are available in the repository

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove all packages installed from the repository along with any packages depending on the packages being removed

subcommands.name: remove-or-distro-sync
subcommands.description: Select all packages installed from the repository. Upgrade, downgrade or keep those of them that are available in another repository to match the latest version available there and remove the others along with any packages depending on the packages being removed

subcommands.name: remove-or-reinstall
subcommands.description: Select all packages installed from the repository. Reinstall those of them that are available in another repository and remove the others along with any packages depending on the packages being removed

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Update all packages to the highest resolvable version available in the repository

subcommands.name: upgrade-to
subcommands.description: A deprecated alias for the upgrade subcommand

name: search
se
subcommands.description: Search package metadata for keywords

options.name: --all
options.description: Lists packages that match at least one of the keys

name: shell
sh
subcommands.description: Open an interactive shell for conducting multiple commands during a single execution of DNF

subcommands.name: swap
subcommands.description: Remove spec and install spec in one transaction

name: updateinfo
upif
subcommands.description: Display information about update advisories

options.name: --summary
options.description: Diplays counts of advisory types

options.name: --list
options.description: List of advisories

options.name: --info
options.description: Detailed information

name: upgrade
up
subcommands.description: Updates the given package(s) (or all if none is given) to the latest version

name: upgrade-minimal
up-min
subcommands.description: Updates package(s) (or all if none is given) to the latest available version that provides a bugfix, enhancement, or a fix for a security issue

options.description: Resolve to IPv4 addresses only
Resolve to IPv6 addresses only
